# Step 12 â€” Each Effector on the Path to the Outcome {#step12}

For ADCD we found no independent contribution to `r cfg$outcome_label` once FcÎ³R3b was accounted for.
Here we apply the same test to ADCP and ADNP: for each effector, antigen, arm and mode, does it add
variance in `r cfg$outcome_label` **beyond antibody quantity (IgG1)** and **beyond IgG1 + FcÎ³R**, and
does it survive **jointly with FcÎ³R3b**? An effector that adds nothing beyond FcÎ³R, or that drops out
against FcÎ³R3b, is not independently on the path.

```{r step12-run, results='asis'}
.stage(12, "each effector on the path to the outcome (ADCD / ADCP / ADNP)")
if (!RUN_EFF_SBA) {
  cat("*Step 12 skipped (run_effector_to_sba = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  dR2 <- function(d, y, add, base) {
    b <- lm(reformulate(base, response = y), data = d)
    f <- lm(reformulate(c(base, add), response = y), data = d)
    ff <- summary(f)$fstatistic
    p <- tryCatch(anova(b, f)$`Pr(>F)`[2], error = function(e) NA)
    list(dR2 = summary(f)$r.squared - summary(b)$r.squared, p = p)
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    rows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      for (A in PATHWAY_ANTIGENS) {
        eff <- paste0(A, "_", fn); igg1 <- paste0(A, "_IgG1")
        r2a <- paste0(A, "_FcgR2a"); r3b <- paste0(A, "_FcgR3b")
        if (!all(c(eff, igg1, r3b, OUTCOME) %in% names(wd))) next
        fcg <- intersect(c(r2a, r3b), names(wd))
        for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
          d <- arm_data_m(mode, m) %>%
            dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(unique(c(OUTCOME, eff, igg1, fcg)))) %>% tidyr::drop_na()
          if (nrow(d) < NETWORK_MIN) next
          inc_q  <- dR2(d, OUTCOME, eff, igg1)              # beyond IgG1 (quantity)
          inc_f  <- dR2(d, OUTCOME, eff, c(igg1, fcg))      # beyond IgG1 + FcÎ³R
          fj <- lm(reformulate(c(r3b, eff), response = OUTCOME), data = d)  # joint with FcÎ³R3b
          cj <- summary(fj)$coefficients
          rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- data.frame(
            effector = fn, antigen = A, arm = m, n = nrow(d),
            "Î”RÂ² eff|IgG1" = round(inc_q$dR2, 3), "p(|IgG1)" = signif(inc_q$p, 3),
            "Î”RÂ² eff|IgG1+FcÎ³R" = round(inc_f$dR2, 3), "p(|IgG1+FcÎ³R)" = signif(inc_f$p, 3),
            "Î² eff (joint w/3b)" = round(cj[eff,1], 3), "p eff" = signif(cj[eff,4], 3),
            "Î² FcÎ³R3b" = round(cj[r3b,1], 3), "p 3b" = signif(cj[r3b,4], 3),
            check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
          .info("step12 [%s] %s %s %s done", mode, fn, A, m)
        }
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (is.null(tb)) { cat("*No complete effector chain in this mode.*\n\n"); next }
    print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Each effector on the path to ", cfg$outcome_label, " [", mode,
      "]: incremental RÂ² beyond IgG1 and beyond IgG1+FcÎ³R, and coefficients in a joint model with FcÎ³R3b."))); cat("\n")
  }
}
```

**How to read this.** `Î”RÂ² eff|IgG1` asks whether the effector tracks `r cfg$outcome_label` at all once
quantity is set; `Î”RÂ² eff|IgG1+FcÎ³R` is the stricter test of an *independent* effector contribution
beyond receptor engagement. In the joint column, an effector that keeps a significant coefficient while
FcÎ³R3b weakens would be genuinely on the path; the ADCD pattern (effector drops, FcÎ³R3b survives) means
the function is a correlate of FcÎ³R3b rather than an independent step. Compare ADCP and ADNP against
ADCD, and TdaP against TT, in the quantity-removed (`igg_std`/`mixed`) modes.
